verdict.label: Worth checking
verdict.headline: Lagide Early morning — 08:00
verdict.reason: Lagide is worth a quick check for early morning — 08:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide helps a little, but it is not a perfect match.

conversationalSummary.text: Lagide at Early morning — 08:00 looks like the best option, but it is still a check-first call. There is enough potential for a useful session, but the lack of push could spoil it. I'd check Surfline or MEO Beachcam before leaving.

best.windFit: S 5 km/h · glassy · good wind fit

wetsuit.reason: Warm water, warm air, and light wind make the 3/2 reasonable.

best.explanation: Lagide looks like a clean fun-sized window for Fun progression, Low confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 93 kJ, 8 s period, consistent 73/100, and glassy conditions.  A dependable left-hand reef that turns modest swell into real rides; this is best for clean, playful walls when the bigger beaches are too wild or too small.  Watch: 6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the reef shelves.
best.shortExplanation: Lagide is worth a quick check for early morning — 08:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide helps a little, but it is not a perfect match.
warnings: 6-9s at mid tide is soft and sectiony on the reef shelves

photo.alt: Photo of Meio da Baia surf spot

wetsuit.reason: Warm water, warm air, and light wind make the 3/2 reasonable.

ranked.explanation: Meio da Baia looks like a clean fun-sized window for Worth checking, Medium confidence: 0.6-1.1 m, 112 kJ, 8 s period, consistent 74/100, and glassy conditions.  An inconsistent Baía spot that can work well when north-northwest swell reaches the bay; this is best for bodyboard sessions when the bay is clean, there is enough period and size, and the forecast shows a defined wave; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable.  Expect: it is less reliable than the main bay spots, so weak swell or short period can make it feel soft.
ranked.shortExplanation: Meio da Baia is worth a quick check for early morning — 08:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.

ranked.explanation: Cerro looks like a clean fun-sized window for Worth checking, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 71 kJ, 8 s period, consistent 71/100, and glassy conditions.  A sheltered beachbreak with gentle lefts and rights, somewhat similar to Cantinho but less consistent; this is best for manageable bodyboard waves when north-northwest swell reaches the bay with clean southerly wind; 6-9s at mid tide can make exposed beaches like Belgas / Almagreira / Cerro / Meio da Baia sharp and rippable.  Watch: Cerro may feel too soft without more push.
ranked.shortExplanation: Cerro is worth a quick check for early morning — 08:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  The wind should help keep it tidy.  The tide and timing are lining up well for this spot.
warnings: Cerro may feel too soft without more push

photo.alt: Photo of Rey Cortiço surf spot

wetsuit.reason: Warm water, warm air, and light wind make the 3/2 reasonable.

ranked.explanation: Rey Cortiço looks like a fun-sized north coast window for Rare window, Medium confidence: 0.6-0.9 m, 71 kJ, 8 s period, workable consistency 56/100, and wind is the main risk.  An open beach break where the sandbanks decide everything; this is best for quiet, punchy peaks when Lagide is too small; 6-9s at low tide can still work on Belgas if the sandbars are lined up, so checking exposed beaches makes sense.  Watch: wind angle is poor for this spot.
ranked.shortExplanation: Rey Cortiço is worth a quick check for lunch — 13:00, but it is not a guaranteed session.  Treat it as a check-first call rather than a sure thing.  Wind is the main thing to watch.  The tide should let the sandbars work without overdoing it.
warnings: wind angle is poor for this spot
